description: High entropy strings containing base64-encoded and random-looking payloads that decode to malicious commands

primary_detection.name: High Entropy String Detected
primary_detection.description: String with entropy >7.0 bits per character detected, indicating potential encoded/encrypted malicious payload
indicators: ENCRYPTED_CONFIG: Base64-encoded string (entropy ~5.8 bits, but suspicious pattern)
SECURE_TOKEN: High entropy alphanumeric string (entropy ~5.9 bits)
AUTH_KEY: Extended high entropy string (entropy ~5.7 bits)

underlying_threats.name: Dynamic Code Execution
underlying_threats.description: exec() called on decoded base64 content

underlying_threats.name: Data Exfiltration
underlying_threats.description: Curl commands sending data to external endpoints

verdict_reasoning: Multiple high-entropy strings combined with exec() on decoded content and data exfiltration via curl confirms credential harvesting malware
recommended_actions: Analyze all base64 strings for hidden payloads
Check for network connections to suspicious domains
